destinations.evidence: The regular transaction execution path consults parent-state AsyncJobTracker-backed sender-lock logic and skips both true results and registry read errors.
The helper implements the same allow/skip cases as the invariant: no jobs allow, settled or expired jobs allow, existing-job fulfillment allows, otherwise sender-locked transactions skip.
The verifier uses the same contract-backed sender job and existing-job fulfillment semantics, which anchors builder/verifier parity.

equivalence_argument: The payload builder and verifier both use AsyncJobTracker parent/contract state via ritual-async-registry. The builder skips only when the helper says the sender is locked and the tx is not an existing-job fulfillment, and it skips on read errors to avoid producing blocks the verifier would reject.
rationale: The builder now calls should_skip_sender_locked_tx against parent AsyncJobTracker state, and the registry module documents/tests differential equivalence with the verifier's nonce-lock logic. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: This is a builder prefilter; consensus enforcement still depends on the verifier, but divergence would hurt block production reliability.
statement: Payload building must skip a regular transaction when AsyncJobTracker shows the sender has at least one pending job that is not phase1Settled and the transaction hash is not an existing job fulfillment; it must allow transactions when the sender has no jobs, all jobs are phase1Settled, or the transaction is a fulfillment.
suggested_falsification_tests: Mock AsyncJobTracker senderJobs and jobExecutor state for no jobs, all phase1Settled, unsettled non-fulfillment, and unsettled fulfillment cases; assert should_skip_sender_locked_tx matches the verifier predicate and the payload loop skips only the unsettled non-fulfillment case.
summary: Builder sender-lock prefilter mirrors the verifier nonce-lock predicate.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Mock AsyncJobTracker senderJobs and jobExecutor state for no jobs, all phase1Settled, unsettled non-fulfillment, and unsettled fulfillment cases; assert should_skip_sender_locked_tx matches the verifier predicate and the payload loop skips only the unsettled non-fulfillment case.
title: Builder sender lock mirrors verifier
